questions.prompt: Let \(f\) and \(g\) have continuous first and second derivatives, with \(f(x)\le g(x)\) for all real \(x\). Which must be true? I. \(f'(x)\le g'(x)\) for all \(x\). II. \(f''(x)\le g''(x)\) for all \(x\). III. \(\int_0^1f(x)dx\le\int_0^1g(x)dx\).
questions.explanation: Pointwise order is preserved by integration, so III is true. It is not preserved by differentiation: one function may stay below another while having a larger first or second derivative at some points. Hence I and II are not guaranteed.

questions.prompt: Let \(f\) be a polynomial of degree greater than 2. If \(a\ne b\) and \(f(a)=f(b)=1\), which must be true for at least one \(x\) between \(a\) and \(b\)? I. \(f(x)=0\) II. \(f\prime(x)=0\) III. \(f\prime\prime(x)=0\)
questions.explanation: Polynomials are continuous and differentiable, so Rolle's Theorem guarantees a point with \(f\prime(x)=0\); II must hold. questions.prompt: A closed cylindrical can has volume \(16\pi\) cubic inches. What height minimizes the amount of material used?

questions.prompt: The slope of the line normal to \(y=2\ln(\sec x)\) at \(x=\dfrac\pi4\) is
questions.explanation: \(y\prime=2\frac{\sec x\tan x}{\sec x}=2\tan x\). At \(x=\pi/4\), the tangent slope is 2, so the normal slope is the negative reciprocal, \(-1/2\).
